title: Iterators, Generators & Collections
source_outline: Iterables vs iterators: iter() and next() (concept)
Generators recap: yield for streaming values
The collections module: Counter, defaultdict, namedtuple (intro)

artifact_plan.profile_note: Corpus-builder suggested standard (recap/intro scoping); human OVERRODE to deep at HITL#2 for capstone coverage — topic packs 6 distinct tools (iterator protocol, for-loop mechanic, generators, Counter, defaultdict, namedtuple) and the coverage rubric requires all survive the distill. Week budget easily absorbs it (~30/90 after this topic). Corpus ~2460w → deep reading band 1800-2200w is a clean distill.
artifact_plan.diagram_note: Corpus-builder recommended no diagram: the for-loop mechanic is rendered as a try/except code equivalent that teaches the iter/next/StopIteration sequence better than a box-arrow flow for this audience; consistent with 5.1/5.2 code-driven style.

next_assessment.title: Data Structures Portfolio
next_assessment.outcome: The learner can choose and manipulate the right Python data structure for a given data-handling task.
